steps.title: Use vertex form
steps.content: For an upward-opening parabola written as $f(x)=a(x-h)^2+k$, the minimum value is the constant $k$.

steps.title: Complete the square
steps.content: Rewrite $x^2-4x-320$ by completing the square: $$x^2-4x=(x-2)^2-4$$ so $$f(x)=(x-2)^2-4-320=(x-2)^2-324$$

steps.title: Confirm the choice
steps.content: The minimum value is shown as the constant $-324$ in vertex form. Choice B — $f(x)=(x-2)^{2}+(-324)$, $(x-2)^2+(-324)$, shows that value, so B is correct.
